title: rebar shared sub-schemas
description: Reusable $defs referenced by the per-output rebar schemas. Authored once here and $ref'd everywhere (a {path,reason} entry, a dep, a comment, etc.) so the shapes never drift between, e.g., `get-file-impact` and `TicketState.file_impact[]`. Load schemas via rebar.schemas.validator(name), which wires a referencing Registry so these cross-file $refs resolve.

creation_channel.description: The public ingress that produced a ticket's genesis CREATE event: `cli` (the rebar CLI), `mcp` (the MCP server), `python` (the direct library facade), `jira` (Jira-inbound), `import` (NDJSON import), or `unknown` (a legacy CREATE that carried no channel; projection-only, never a valid live-write value).

source_author.description: Provenance (P1.2 import): original comment author from the source store. Present only on imported comments.

source_created_at.description: Provenance (P1.2 import): original comment timestamp from the source store. Present only on imported comments.

input.description: The raw input that failed to resolve.

exit_code.description: Optional: the process exit code that accompanied this failure (see docs/exit-codes.md). Present on emitted failure envelopes; omitted by legacy read-not-found callers.

severity.description: Finding severity, highest→lowest. 'info' is a non-actionable observation.

citation.description: Evidence backing a finding (rebar.llm). The `kind` discriminator selects the shape: a repo file location (path [, line_start, line_end]); a URL; or a freeform source/evidence description. `kind` is required so consumers can branch; the other fields are populated per kind.

line_end.description: 1-based end line (kind=file); 0/omitted means whole file (i.e. through the end).

description.description: Freeform source/evidence description (kind=source), or extra context for any kind.

finding.description: One reviewer finding — the universal output element of every LLM review operation (rebar.llm).

dimension.description: Category/dimension the finding belongs to (e.g. 'security', 'acceptance-criteria', 'test-coverage'). Reviewer-defined.

reviewer_id.description: Id of the reviewer prompt that produced this finding.
